data.title: HawkEye 360 launches IPO roadshow
data.description:
• HawkEye 360 (HAWK) said on Monday it has begun the roadshow for its initial public offering of 16 million shares of common stock, with an expected price range of $24 to $26 per share.
  • The company plans to grant underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.4 million shares.
  • HawkEye 360 intends to list its shares on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker “HAWK.”
  • The offering is subject to market conditions, and the registration statement filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has not yet become effective.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Peloton workouts now available to Spotify Premium subscribers
data.description:
• Spotify’s (SPOT) premium subscribers can now take Peloton (PTON) workout classes through a partnership between the streaming music service and fitness provider.
  • The partnership will bring a catalog of more than 1,400 ad-free and on-demand Peloton (PTON) classes to Spotify’s (SPOT) service, including outdoor runs and structured mat classes.
  • “With this new genre, we’re opening the door to a broader range of fitness and wellness experiences, all designed to be flexible, accessible, and easy to integrate into your day,” Spotify said on their website.
  • Peloton classes on Spotify are primarily in English, with select options in German and Spanish.
  • The partnership is giving a boost to Peloton (PTON) with shares up more than 7% in Monday’s premarket trading. Spotify (SPOT) shares were unchanged.

data.title: Sranan Gold plans C$3M private placement
data.description:
• Sranan Gold (SRANF) on Monday said it plans a non-brokered private placement of as many as 20M units at C$0.15 each to raise gross proceeds of up to C$3M.
  • The offering is being led by strategic investor Concept Capital Management, which committed C$800,000, the company said.
  • Each unit will include one common share and one-half of a warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable at C$0.275 per share for 48 months from issuance.
  • Sranan said it expects the financing to close on or before May 31, subject to customary conditions and approval from the Canadian Securities Exchange.
  • The company plans to use proceeds for drilling and exploration at its Tapanahony Project in Suriname, work on the recently acquired Lawatino concession, as well as working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Musk, Altman's court battle may take 'significantly' more time to settle: Wedbush
data.description:
As Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI (OPENAI) starts on Monday with jury selection, Wedbush (IVES) believes the case may take significantly more time to reach a resolution, with Musk taking this legal battle more personally and looking to battle OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman and the startup.

On Friday, a California federal judge dismissed fraud claims alleged by Tesla's (TSLA) CEO Musk that OpenAI, the AI startup he helped cofound, abandoned its founding mission to operate as a non-profit organization. However, a trial will still start on Monday to address Musk's claims of breach of charitable trust and unjust ​enrichment against OpenAI and co-founder Altman. Reportedly, Musk is seeking $150B in damages, and if successful, the proceeds would go to OpenAI's charitable division.

"The biggest focus of the trial will be around if OpenAI broke its charitable mission when they spun off its for-profit arm and accepted an investment from Microsoft (MSFT) for its AI technology, which was a promise to Musk in theory that would not happen," said analysts led by Dan Ives.

The analysts noted that as this battle plays out in the court, the ripple effects of the trial will be a focus across the AI landscape. "With OpenAI set to go through a record-breaking IPO in the back half of this year, this presents an overhang/thorn in the side to the company." the analsyts added.

The analysts said that the lawsuit also heightens the competitive landscape within the AI Large Language Model, or LLM, world, with OpenAI and Musk's xAI (X.AI) going head-to-head to gain share in the AI arms race, and OpenAI has a strong market position, which poses a risk to other tech players including Musk.

"With Musk’s existing lawsuits against OpenAI, Altman and Musk’s feud continues to heat up as this battle heads to court with the judges increasingly focused on whether OpenAI executives committed fraud against its initial operating structure," said Ives and his team.

Earlier this month, OpenAI sent a letter to the California and Delaware attorneys general, asking them to investigate "improper and anti-competitive behavior" by Musk and his associates.

Musk had sued OpenAI in 2024, accusing it of violating OpenAI's founding mission as it ​restructures to a for-profit entity. Altman and Musk cofounded the AI startup in 2015, but Musk left in 2018. Musk later ⁠launched rival xAI (X.AI), which created the AI chatbot Grok. Musk is asking for a court order to change OpenAI's status back to a non-profit and also that Altman and President Greg Brockman be removed from their positions.

"We believe any major damage to OpenAI and Altman will be more scrapes and bruises than real consequences to the company and his role as CEO. That said, it’s Elon and never doubt him in these spots. While there will likely a settlement/resolution in the case, we believe the case may take significantly more time to reach a resolution with Musk taking this legal battle more personally and looking to truly battle Altman/OpenAI," said Ives and his team.

data.title: Strategy buys 3,273 BTC for $255M at $77,906 average price from Apr 20–26
data.description:
• Strategy Inc (MSTR) acquired 3,273 BTC for $255M at an average price of $77,906 during the period April 20, 2026, to April 26, 2026.
  • As of April 26, 2026: Total holdings stand at 818,334 BTC, with an aggregate purchase value of $61.81 billion and an average cost basis of $75,537.
  • The bitcoin (BTC-USD) purchases were made using proceeds from the sale of shares under the ATM.

data.title: Flexsteel to repurchase $60M in shares from founding family
data.description:
• Flexsteel Industries (FLXS) on Monday said it has agreed to repurchase about 1.28 million shares, or roughly 24% of its outstanding stock, from members of the Bertsch family for approximately $60.2 million.
  • The shares will be bought at $47.00 each, representing a 2.5% discount to the April 24 closing price, in a privately negotiated transaction.
  • The furniture maker said the buyback will be funded through a mix of existing cash and borrowings under its revolving credit facility, and is expected to close on April 28, 2026.
  • Following the transaction, director Brooks Bertsch will resign from the company’s board.
  • FLXS -3.92% after hours to $46.31.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Thermo Fisher to sell microbiology unit to Astorg for $1.08B
data.description:
• Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) on Monday said it has agreed to sell its microbiology business to private equity firm Astorg for about $1.075 billion in cash and a $50 million seller note.
  • The unit, which provides antimicrobial susceptibility testing and culture media solutions, generated $645 million in revenue in 2025 and is part of Thermo Fisher’s specialty diagnostics segment.
  • The company expects the deal to close in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and customary conditions.
  • Thermo Fisher said the transaction is expected to be dilutive to adjusted earnings per share by $0.15 in the first full year after closing.
  • TMO -0.12% premarket to $469.12.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Comfort Systems USA leads all large-cap industrial stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap industrial stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sub-sectors within industrials, including Construction and Engineering, Aerospace and Defense, Industrial Machinery & Supplies & Components, Electrical Components and Equipment, Heavy Electrical Equipment, and Research and Consulting Services.

Comfort Systems USA (FIX) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 158.63% year-over-year, reflecting aggressive investment in its HVAC and mechanical contracting operations amid booming demand driven by data center and industrial construction.

SPX Technologies (SPXC) and Rocket Lab Corporation (RKLB) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 142.37% and 132.94% respectively, while IES Holdings (IESC) and Carpenter Technology (CRS) round out the top five at 94.27% and 77.25%.

Vertiv Holdings (VRT) and MasTec (MTZ) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 76.57% and 74.66%, while GE Vernova (GEV) and Planet Labs (PL) follow at 74.65% and 73.18%.

Axon Enterprise (AXON) rounds out the list at 72.95%.

A standout quant signal emerges from the middle of the rankings: MasTec (MTZ) is the only Strong Buy on the list with a Quant Rating of 4.72, while SPX Technologies (SPXC) carries the sole Buy rating at 4.14, making these two names the most favored by the quant model despite neither leading in raw CapEx growth. The remaining eight stocks all carry Hold ratings ranging from 2.63 to 3.49.

Here is the list:

  1. Comfort Systems USA, Inc. (FIX), CapEx growth YoY: 158.63%

  2. SPX Technologies, Inc. (SPXC), CapEx growth YoY: 142.37%

  3. Rocket Lab Corporation (RKLB), CapEx growth YoY: 132.94%

  4. IES Holdings, Inc. (IESC), CapEx growth YoY: 94.27%

  5. Carpenter Technology Corporation (CRS), CapEx growth YoY: 77.25%

  6. Vertiv Holdings Co (VRT), CapEx growth YoY: 76.57%

  7. MasTec, Inc. (MTZ), CapEx growth YoY: 74.66%

  8. GE Vernova Inc. (GEV), CapEx growth YoY: 74.65%

  9. Planet Labs PBC (PL), CapEx growth YoY: 73.18%

  10. Axon Enterprise, Inc. (AXON), CapEx growth YoY: 72.95%

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN)

data.title: Youxin Technology jumps over 40% on $10.8M deal for YATOP stake
Dell, HP Enterprise in focus as BofA ups price targets on agentic AI boost
data.description:
Dell Technologies (DELL) and HP Enterprise (HPE) were in focus on Monday as Bank of America upped its price targets on the pair, citing a boost from agentic artificial intelligence.

“The next phase of AI is agentic with multistep workloads requiring more CPU intensive hardware, storage access, and execution systems,” analysts at the firm, led by Wamsi Mohan, wrote in a note to clients. “Agentic AI turns one discrete inferencing event into sequenced workflows, driving more inference events per task, and an increase in persistent workloads driving more overall infrastructure spend in AI servers, ISS, and storage. The importance of agentic AI for Dell and HPE is increasing demand for AI servers and storage but especially their traditional server business. Dell has been selling traditional servers to NeoClouds and we expect the momentum of traditional servers to improve over the next few years.”

Dell's price target was raised to $246 from $205, while HP Enterprise's target was lifted to $38 from $32.

Delving deeper, the analysts said they see Dell and HP Enterprise gaining share in the server market, amid the pickup in demand caused by agentic AI.

“The shift towards agentic AI benefits AI server demand first as inference frequency is increased and model usage becomes a persistent production workload,” the analysts added. “We see Dell as a leading OEM in AI servers, with 12% share of total AI server revenues of $496bn in 2026 while share gain velocity at Neo Clouds is increasing (potentially ~$10bn this year). While HPE is shifting focus towards networking given Juniper (JNPR) acquisition, they still benefit as a premium AI server OEM (we estimate $6.5bn of AI server revs in C26).”
